protocol_definition.name: Manager-Worker
protocol_definition.message_flow: Manager assigns work, workers report, Manager resolves conflicts, Writer finalizes.
protocol_definition.visibility: Manager sees all messages; workers see the task, assignment, and relevant summaries.
protocol_definition.termination: Stop after Manager synthesis and Writer output or budget exhaustion.

final_call.expected_action: Produce the self-contained final deliverable after independently checking the Manager decision against every task constraint and verified worker result.

intermediate_messages.expected_action: Decompose the task and assign distinct subtasks to Planner, Researcher, Analyst, Critic, and Writer.
intermediate_messages.content:
**Manager: Final Strategy Document**

---

## 1. Executive Summary

This 12-week strategy pilots an AI-powered customer-support assistant for a mid-sized e-commerce company handling 50,000 monthly requests. The pilot aims to reduce first-response time from 18 hours to ≤8 hours, raise customer satisfaction from 78% to ≥84%, and reduce repetitive agent workload—all within a $180,000 budget and strict safety/privacy constraints. The plan phases preparation, development, pilot, and decision-making, with built-in responses to two potential disruptions: a 20% budget cut at Week 6 and a satisfaction shortfall at Week 9. A final go/no-go decision is made by Week 12.

## 2. Strategic Objectives and Priorities

1. **Speed & Satisfaction**: Achieve ≤8-hour first response and ≥84% CSAT.
2. **Agent Relief**: Automate ≥40% of repetitive queries (e.g., order status, return policies).
3. **Safety & Compliance**: Never expose payment-card data; never auto-approve refunds, account changes, or payment disputes.
4. **Budget Discipline**: Spend exactly $180,000; prioritize essential infrastructure if reduced.
5. **Evidence-Based Decision**: Use pilot data to launch, revise, or cancel by Week 12.

## 3. Assumptions and Scope Boundaries

**Assumptions:**
- Existing support ticketing system has API access for integration.
- Historical data (anonymized) is available for model training.
- Agents can be trained on new tool within 2 hours/week.
- Privacy/Legal specialist can review outputs within 0.25 FTE.
- Budget reduction means cutting non-essential items; core development continues.

**Scope Boundaries:**
- AI may draft responses and recommend actions only.
- No independent refunds, account modifications, or payment dispute resolution.
- Payment-card numbers stripped from all model inputs.
- Pilot limited to 8 agents, 2 hours/week each.
- Pilot must start by end of Week 8; final decision by end of Week 12.

## 4. Twelve-Week Phased Roadmap

| Phase & Weeks | Main Activities | Deliverables | Responsible Roles | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Phase 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-3)** | Define scope; collect & anonymize 10,000 historical tickets; build data pipeline; draft safety rules; select AI model (e.g., fine-tuned LLM). | Data pipeline; safety rules document; model selection report. | PM, ML Eng, Backend Eng, Data Analyst, Privacy/Legal | Data pipeline tested; safety rules approved; model selected. |
| **Phase 2: Development (Weeks 4-6)** | Train model on anonymized data; build API for draft responses; integrate with ticketing system; create agent dashboard; unit tests. | Trained model; integration API; agent dashboard v1; test results. | ML Eng, Backend Eng, PM, Data Analyst | Model accuracy ≥80% on test set; integration passes security review. |
| **Phase 3: Pilot Prep (Weeks 7-8)** | Train 8 agents (2 hrs each); set up monitoring; define escalation paths; run internal dry-run with synthetic queries. | Agent training completed; monitoring dashboard; dry-run report. | PM, Support Lead, ML Eng, Privacy/Legal | Dry-run shows no safety violations; agents comfortable with tool. |
| **Phase 4: Pilot (Weeks 9-10)** | Live pilot with 8 agents handling ~5,000 requests/month; collect metrics; weekly reviews. | Weekly metric reports; agent feedback logs. | PM, Data Analyst, Support Lead, ML Eng | Pilot runs for 2 full weeks; data collected. |
| **Phase 5: Decision (Weeks 11-12)** | Analyze pilot data; compare to thresholds; prepare recommendation; final go/no-go decision. | Final analysis report; decision document. | PM, Data Analyst, Privacy/Legal, ML Eng | Decision made by end of Week 12. |

## 5. Team Responsibilities

- **Product Manager (1 FTE)**: Overall coordination, stakeholder communication, budget tracking, decision framework.
- **Machine Learning Engineer (1 FTE)**: Model training, evaluation, safety guardrails, performance tuning.
- **Backend Engineer (1 FTE)**: API development, system integration, data pipeline, security implementation.
- **Data Analyst (0.5 FTE)**: Metric collection, statistical analysis, dashboard creation, pilot reporting.
- **Customer Support Lead (0.5 FTE)**: Agent training, workflow design, feedback collection, escalation management.
- **Privacy & Legal Specialist (0.25 FTE)**: Compliance review, data anonymization audit, safety rule approval.

## 6. Budget Allocation

| Budget Category | Amount | Purpose | Priority if Budget Reduced |
|---|---|---|---|
| Cloud Compute & Model Training | $60,000 | GPU/TPU time, model hosting, data storage | High (essential for development) |
| Engineering Salaries (ML + Backend) | $70,000 | 12 weeks of 2 engineers | High (core team) |
| PM & Analyst Salaries | $30,000 | 12 weeks of PM + 0.5 analyst | Medium (can reduce analyst hours) |
| Agent Training & Testing Stipends | $10,000 | 8 agents × 2 hrs/week × 12 weeks | Low (can reduce to 1 hr/week) |
| Privacy/Legal Consulting | $5,000 | 0.25 FTE for 12 weeks | Low (can defer some reviews) |
| Contingency Reserve | $5,000 | Unforeseen costs | Medium (use only if needed) |
| **Total** | **$180,000** | | |

*If budget reduced 20% ($36,000 cut):* Cut Contingency ($5,000), reduce Agent Stipends to $5,000, cut Analyst to 0.25 FTE (saving $15,000), reduce Engineering by $11,000 (e.g., fewer cloud credits).

## 7. Pilot Design

- **Scale**: 8 agents handle ~5,000 requests/month (10% of volume) during Weeks 9-10.
- **Process**: AI drafts response → agent reviews/edits → sends. Agent logs satisfaction with draft.
- **Safety**: Model inputs stripped of payment-card data via regex + PII detection. All refund/account/payment disputes routed to human-only queue.
- **Monitoring**: Daily CSAT surveys, response-time tracking, agent workload logs, safety violation alerts.
- **Duration**: 2 weeks live (Weeks 9-10) with data collection; analysis in Weeks 11-12.

## 8. Success Metrics and Decision Thresholds

| Metric | Baseline | Target | Measurement Method | Go/No-Go Threshold |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Avg First-Response Time | 18 hours | ≤8 hours | Ticketing system timestamps | ≤8 hours (Go); >8 hours (No-Go) |
| Customer Satisfaction | 78% | ≥84% | Post-interaction survey (1-5 scale) | ≥84% (Go); <84% (No-Go) |
| Repetitive Query Automation | 0% | ≥40% | % of queries where AI draft used without major edits | ≥40% (Go); <40% (No-Go) |
| Safety Incidents | 0 | 0 | Manual audit of all AI-drafted responses | 0 incidents (Go); any incident (No-Go) |
| Agent Time Saved | 0 | ≥2 hrs/week/agent | Self-reported time logs | ≥2 hrs (Go); <2 hrs (No-Go) |

**Go/No-Go Rule**: All five thresholds must be met for launch. If 3-4 met, consider limited extension/revision. If ≤2 met, cancel.

## 9. Risk Register

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Early Warning Sign | Mitigation | Contingency Owner |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Model produces unsafe responses | Medium | High | Safety audit flags errors | Strict guardrails; human-in-loop; weekly audits | ML Eng |
| Budget cut at Week 6 | High | Medium | Finance notification | Pre-planned cost reductions (see budget) | PM |
| Pilot fails CSAT target | Medium | High | Weekly CSAT <80% by Week 9 | Analyze root cause; extend pilot 1 week; adjust model | Data Analyst |
| Data privacy breach | Low | Critical | PII detected in model inputs | Strip PII pre-input; encrypt data; legal review | Privacy/Legal |
| Agent resistance to tool | Medium | Medium | Low adoption in first week | Training; feedback sessions; show time savings | Support Lead |
| Integration delays | Medium | High | API testing fails | Buffer 1 week in Phase 2; use simpler integration | Backend Eng |
| Model accuracy <80% | Medium | High | Test set performance low | More training data; fine-tuning; simpler model | ML Eng |
| Pilot scale too small for statistical significance | Low | Medium | High variance in metrics | Use Bayesian analysis; extend pilot to 3 weeks if needed | Data Analyst |

## 10. Disruption-Response Plans

**Disruption 1: Budget reduced 20% at end of Week 6**
- **Immediate action**: PM notifies team; implement pre-planned cuts (see Budget section).
- **Impact**: Reduce cloud compute (use smaller model), cut analyst to 0.25 FTE, reduce agent training to 1 hr/week.
- **Timeline**: Adjustments made by Week 7 start; no delay to pilot start.
- **Contingency**: If cuts threaten model quality, use pre-trained model instead of fine-tuning.

**Disruption 2: Pilot shows faster response times but CSAT <84% at end of Week 9**
- **Immediate action**: Data Analyst runs root-cause analysis (e.g., generic responses, errors).
- **Response**: Extend pilot by 1 week (Week 10 becomes Week 11); adjust model tone/persona; add agent training on AI editing.
- **Decision**: If CSAT improves to ≥84% by end of extended pilot, proceed to launch. If not, recommend revision (e.g., more agent training) or cancellation.
- **Timeline**: Final decision moved to end of Week 12 (original deadline maintained).

## 11. Final Decision Framework

| Condition | Decision | Action |
|---|---|---|
| All 5 metrics meet thresholds | **Launch** | Full rollout plan; allocate remaining budget for scaling |
| 3-4 metrics met (including safety) | **Limited Extension or Revision** | 4-week extension to fix gaps; re-evaluate with new data |
| ≤2 metrics met, or any safety incident | **Cancel** | Document lessons; archive model; reallocate team |
| Budget cut caused metric failure | **Revision** | Seek additional funding or scope reduction; re-pilot |

**Decision Authority**: PM, with sign-off from Privacy/Legal and Support Lead.

## 12. Conclusion

This 12-week strategy balances speed, quality, safety, and budget. By phasing preparation, development, pilot, and decision-making, the team can deliver evidence-based results by Week 12. Built-in disruption responses ensure resilience. The final decision framework provides clear criteria for launch, revision, or cancellation, protecting the company from unsafe or ineffective deployment.

intermediate_messages.expected_action: Complete the Planner assignment. Report evidence, assumptions, and unresolved issues to Manager.
intermediate_messages.content:
**Evidence:**

- The task explicitly states a 12-week timeline, $180,000 budget, and specific performance baselines (18-hour first response, 78% CSAT) and targets (≤8 hours, ≥84% CSAT).
- Constraints are clearly listed: no independent refunds/account changes/payment disputes; no payment-card data in model inputs; pilot must start by end of Week 8; final decision by end of Week 12.
- Team composition and agent availability (8 agents, 2 hours/week each) are fixed.
- Two disruptions are specified: 20% budget cut at Week 6, and CSAT failure at Week 9.

**Assumptions (labeled):**

- The existing ticketing system has an API for integration (required for the roadmap's Phase 2).
- Historical ticket data is available and can be anonymized for training (required for Phase 1).
- The chosen AI model (e.g., fine-tuned LLM) can achieve ≥80% accuracy on test data within the budget and timeline.
- The 0.25 FTE Privacy/Legal specialist can review outputs within the 12-week period.
- Agent training (2 hours/week) is sufficient for them to use the tool effectively.
- The budget reduction plan (cutting contingency, agent stipends, analyst hours, and cloud credits) is feasible without halting core development.

**Unresolved Issues:**

- The exact AI model to be used is not specified; the plan assumes a fine-tuned LLM, but this may need adjustment based on cost and performance.
- The pilot scale (5,000 requests/month) is an assumption; the task does not specify the exact volume for the pilot, only that it must be "limited" and start by Week 8.
- The "repetitive query automation" metric (≥40%) is a target set by the plan; the task only says "reduce repetitive workload," not a specific percentage.
- The budget allocation for salaries ($70,000 for engineers, $30,000 for PM/analyst) is an estimate; actual costs may vary based on company pay scales.
- The disruption response for CSAT failure assumes a 1-week pilot extension is feasible within the 12-week timeline; this may compress the decision phase.

**Recommendation to Manager:** The plan is internally consistent and addresses all constraints. The unresolved issues are minor and can be resolved during Phase 1 (e.g., model selection, pilot volume). The budget reduction plan and CSAT failure response are pre-defined, but the manager should confirm the salary estimates and pilot volume assumptions before final approval.

intermediate_messages.content:
**Researcher Report for Manager**

**Task ID:** SP-04
**Role:** Researcher

**Evidence Gathered and Verified**

The following evidence is extracted directly from the provided prompt and prior context. All facts are verified against the source; no external data or invented results are included.

**Verified Facts (from prompt):**
- Company: Mid-sized e-commerce, 50,000 support requests/month.
- Current performance: First-response time = 18 hours; CSAT = 78%.
- Goals: Reduce first-response time to ≤8 hours; increase CSAT to ≥84%; reduce repetitive workload; avoid unsafe/unauthorized actions; protect customer/payment info.
- Constraints: Budget = $180,000; duration = 12 weeks; pilot must begin by end of Week 8; final decision by end of Week 12.
- System limitations: May draft responses and recommend actions only; may not independently issue refunds, modify accounts, or resolve payment disputes. Payment-card info must not be in model inputs.
- Team: 1 PM, 1 ML Engineer, 1 Backend Engineer, 0.5 Data Analyst, 0.5 Support Lead, 0.25 Privacy/Legal Specialist.
- Agent participation: 8 agents, max 2 hours/week each.
- Disruption 1: At end of Week 6, budget may be reduced by 20%.
- Disruption 2: At end of Week 9, pilot may show faster response times but fail CSAT target.

**Verified Facts (from prior context - Manager's Final Strategy Document):**
- The provided strategy document is the Manager's assignment output, not a completed pilot result. It is a plan, not evidence of execution.
- Budget table totals exactly $180,000 with categories: Cloud Compute ($60k), Engineering Salaries ($70k), PM & Analyst Salaries ($30k), Agent Training ($10k), Privacy/Legal ($5k), Contingency ($5k).
- Success metrics table includes five metrics with baselines and targets as stated in prompt.
- Risk register includes eight risks; six are explicitly required by prompt (at least six), but eight are provided.
- Decision framework includes four conditions: Launch, Limited Extension/Revision, Cancel, and a fourth for budget-cut failure.

**Assumptions Identified (labeled as such):**
1. Existing support ticketing system has API access for integration. (Not stated in prompt; necessary for plan feasibility.)
2. Historical data (anonymized) is available for model training. (Not stated; assumed for development phase.)
3. Agents can be trained on new tool within 2 hours/week. (Matches agent constraint; assumed sufficient.)
4. Privacy/Legal specialist can review outputs within 0.25 FTE. (Assumed capacity; not verified.)
5. Budget reduction means cutting non-essential items; core development continues. (Assumed prioritization logic.)
6. Model accuracy ≥80% on test set is a reasonable development milestone. (Not specified in prompt; assumed by strategy.)
7. Pilot scale of 5,000 requests/month (10% of volume) is sufficient for statistical significance. (Assumed; not validated.)
8. Bayesian analysis can compensate for small sample size if needed. (Assumed statistical method.)

**Unresolved Issues and Gaps:**
1. **No explicit definition of "repetitive queries"** in prompt. The strategy assumes ≥40% automation target, but the prompt only says "reduce repetitive workload." The 40% figure is an assumption, not a given.
2. **Agent time saved metric** (≥2 hrs/week/agent) is not specified in prompt goals. It is an inferred metric from "reduce repetitive workload." The prompt does not quantify this.
3. **Budget reduction impact on model quality** is not fully addressed. The strategy assumes cutting cloud compute and analyst hours, but the prompt does not specify whether a smaller model or reduced data analysis would still meet goals. This is a risk.
4. **Disruption 2 response** (CSAT failure at Week 9) proposes extending pilot by 1 week, but the prompt requires final decision by end of Week 12. The strategy maintains this deadline, but the extension may compress analysis time. Feasibility is uncertain.
5. **No explicit contingency for integration delays** beyond a 1-week buffer. The prompt does not guarantee API availability; this is a dependency.
6. **Safety incident definition** is binary (0 incidents = Go; any = No-Go). The prompt does not define what constitutes a "safety incident" (e.g., near-miss vs. actual breach). This could lead to ambiguity.
7. **Agent resistance risk** is listed but mitigation (training, feedback) is generic. No specific metrics for agent adoption are in the success metrics table.

**Summary of Evidence for Decision:**
- The strategy is internally consistent with all prompt constraints and goals.
- All required tables are present and populated with plausible values.
- The budget sums to exactly $180,000.
- The roadmap respects the Week 8 pilot start and Week 12 decision deadline.
- Disruption plans are outlined but rely on assumptions about team flexibility and model performance.
- The final decision framework is clear and aligns with prompt requirements.

**Recommendation to Manager:**
The strategy is sound as a plan. However, the unresolved issues above should be addressed before execution, particularly: (a) define "repetitive queries" operationally, (b) clarify safety incident thresholds, and (c) validate that a 1-week pilot extension is feasible within the 12-week window without compromising analysis quality. The budget reduction plan is reasonable but assumes the team can absorb cuts without critical failure—this should be stress-tested.

intermediate_messages.expected_action: Complete the Analyst assignment. Report evidence, assumptions, and unresolved issues to Manager.
intermediate_messages.content:
**Analyst Report for Manager – Task SP-04**

**1. Budget Verification**
- **Claimed total:** $180,000.
- **Line items sum:** $60,000 + $70,000 + $30,000 + $10,000 + $5,000 + $5,000 = $180,000. **Confirmed.**
- **Budget reduction scenario (20% = $36,000):** Proposed cuts total $5,000 (contingency) + $5,000 (agent stipends) + $15,000 (analyst reduction) + $11,000 (engineering/cloud) = $36,000. **Confirmed.** However, the $11,000 engineering cut is vaguely attributed to “fewer cloud credits.” This is a risk: cloud compute is already only $60,000. Cutting $11,000 from that line would reduce it to $49,000, which may degrade model training. **Flag:** The plan should specify that the $11,000 cut comes from reducing GPU hours, not from engineering salaries, to avoid losing core staff.

**2. Timeline Feasibility Check**
- **Constraint:** Pilot must begin by end of Week 8. The roadmap shows Phase 3 (Pilot Prep) ending Week 8, with Phase 4 (Pilot) starting Week 9. **Compliant.**
- **Constraint:** Final decision by end of Week 12. Phase 5 ends Week 12. **Compliant.**
- **Disruption 2 response:** Extending pilot by 1 week (Week 10 becomes Week 11) pushes analysis to Weeks 11-12. The final decision remains Week 12. **Feasible, but tight.** The plan assumes no additional delays from root-cause analysis. **Assumption noted.**

**3. Metric Thresholds and Statistical Validity**
- **Pilot scale:** 8 agents handling ~5,000 requests/month over 2 weeks = ~2,500 requests. For a CSAT target of ≥84% (baseline 78%), a sample of 2,500 yields a margin of error of approximately ±1.5% at 95% confidence. This is sufficient to detect a 6-point improvement. **Statistically valid.**
- **Agent time saved:** Target is ≥2 hrs/week/agent. With 8 agents and 2 weeks, total saved hours = 32 hours. Self-reported logs are subjective. **Recommendation:** Cross-validate with ticketing system data (e.g., time between ticket assignment and agent response). Not currently in plan.

**4. Safety and Privacy Constraints**
- **Constraint:** Payment-card information must not be included in model inputs. Plan includes regex + PII detection. **Adequate.**
- **Constraint:** AI may not independently issue refunds, modify accounts, or resolve payment disputes. Plan routes these to human-only queue. **Compliant.**
- **Risk:** The plan does not specify how the system will detect and block payment-card data that is not in standard format (e.g., handwritten notes). **Assumption:** Regex covers common formats. **Flag:** Add manual audit of a random 5% of inputs during pilot.

**5. Resource Constraints**
- **Team:** 1 PM, 1 ML Eng, 1 Backend Eng, 0.5 Data Analyst, 0.5 Support Lead, 0.25 Privacy/Legal. **All accounted for.**
- **Agent hours:** 8 agents × 2 hrs/week × 12 weeks = 192 hours. Budget allocates $10,000 for stipends. At $10,000 / 192 hours = ~$52/hour. **Reasonable.**
- **Privacy/Legal at 0.25 FTE:** 10 hours/week. For 12 weeks = 120 hours. This is sufficient for initial review and weekly audits. **Adequate.**

**6. Unsupported Facts and Assumptions**
- **Assumption:** Existing ticketing system has API access. **Not stated in constraints.** If no API exists, integration timeline (Weeks 4-6) is unrealistic. **Flag:** This is a critical dependency. Recommend PM verify by end of Week 1.
- **Assumption:** Historical data is available and anonymized. **Not stated.** If data is not available, Phase 1 delays. **Flag:** Add a Week 1 checkpoint.
- **Assumption:** Model accuracy ≥80% on test set is achievable. **Not guaranteed.** The plan does not specify what “accuracy” means (e.g., response relevance, safety compliance). **Recommendation:** Define accuracy as “response deemed acceptable by agent in dry-run” to align with pilot goals.

**7. Risk Register Completeness**
- **Six risks minimum:** The register lists 8 risks. **Compliant.**
- **Missing risk:** The plan does not address the risk that the pilot scale (5,000 requests) may not represent peak volume or seasonal variation. **Assumption:** Pilot volume is representative. **Flag:** Add this as a risk with low likelihood but medium impact.

**8. Final Decision Framework Logic**
- **All 5 metrics met:** Launch. **Clear.**
- **3-4 metrics met (including safety):** Limited extension/revision. **Clear.**
- **≤2 metrics met or any safety incident:** Cancel. **Clear.**
- **Budget cut caused metric failure:** Revision. **Clear.**
- **No ambiguity:** The framework does not specify what happens if safety is met but CSAT and response time fail. The rule “≤2 metrics met” would trigger cancellation. **Consistent.**

**Unresolved Issues for Manager:**
1. **API dependency:** Verify ticketing system API availability by Week 1. If absent, the entire timeline is at risk.
2. **Budget cut specificity:** The $11,000 engineering cut needs a concrete source (e.g., reduce GPU hours, not salaries).
3. **Accuracy definition:** Clarify what “accuracy ≥80%” means for the model test set.
4. **Pilot representativeness:** Consider adding a note that pilot results may not generalize to peak seasons.

**Conclusion:** The strategy is internally consistent, budget-balanced, and compliant with all stated constraints. The two disruption plans are actionable and do not break the timeline. The three flagged issues (API, budget cut detail, accuracy definition) require manager clarification before execution.

intermediate_messages.expected_action: Complete the Critic assignment. Report evidence, assumptions, and unresolved issues to Manager.
intermediate_messages.content:
**Critic Report for Manager**

**Task ID: SP-04**

**1. Budget Discrepancy (Hard Constraint Violation)**
- **Claim:** Total budget is exactly $180,000.
- **Evidence:** The Budget table sums to $180,000 ($60k + $70k + $30k + $10k + $5k + $5k = $180,000). This is arithmetically correct.
- **Issue:** The "Priority if Budget Reduced" column lists "Agent Training & Testing Stipends" as Low priority, but the disruption plan (Section 10) reduces agent training to 1 hr/week. The budget table shows $10,000 for 8 agents × 2 hrs/week × 12 weeks = $10,000. Reducing to 1 hr/week would save $5,000, not the $5,000 cut shown. The math is consistent, but the *purpose* column says "12 weeks" while agents only participate in testing for 2 hours/week for 8 weeks (Weeks 9-10 pilot) plus training in Weeks 7-8. The $10,000 figure assumes 12 weeks of stipends, but agents are only active for ~4 weeks (training + pilot). This overestimates cost by ~$6,667. **Correction:** Recalculate stipends based on actual agent involvement (8 agents × 2 hrs/week × 4 weeks = 64 hours; at $20/hr = $1,280, not $10,000). The budget is inflated.

**2. Pilot Design Scale (Unsupported Assumption)**
- **Claim:** Pilot handles ~5,000 requests/month (10% of volume).
- **Evidence:** 8 agents × 2 hrs/week = 16 agent-hours/week. At 50,000 requests/month, average handle time is unknown. If each request takes 5 minutes, 16 hours = 192 requests/week, or ~768/month—far below 5,000. The 5,000 figure is unsupported and likely unachievable given agent time constraints. **Correction:** Reduce pilot volume to ~800 requests/month or justify handle time assumption.

**3. Success Metric Thresholds (Inconsistent Logic)**
- **Claim:** All five thresholds must be met for launch.
- **Evidence:** The "Go/No-Go Threshold" column lists binary thresholds (e.g., ≤8 hours, ≥84%). However, the "Go/No-Go Rule" says "All five thresholds must be met." This contradicts the disruption plan (Section 10) which allows extension if CSAT fails. The rule should specify that safety is non-negotiable, but other metrics may have conditional thresholds. **Correction:** Clarify that safety is a hard gate; other metrics can trigger revision, not automatic cancellation.

**4. Risk Register Missing Required Minimum**
- **Claim:** At least six risks are required.
- **Evidence:** The Risk Register lists eight risks. This meets the requirement.
- **No issue.**

**5. Disruption 2 Timeline (Logical Error)**
- **Claim:** Disruption at end of Week 9; extend pilot by 1 week; final decision by end of Week 12.
- **Evidence:** Pilot runs Weeks 9-10. If extended by 1 week, pilot ends Week 11. Analysis in Weeks 11-12 still allows decision by Week 12. This is feasible.
- **No issue.**

**6. Assumptions Labeling (Missing Explicit Label)**
- **Claim:** "Clearly label any additional assumptions."
- **Evidence:** Section 3 lists assumptions but does not explicitly state they are *additional* to the prompt. The prompt already provides constraints; the plan adds assumptions (e.g., API access, historical data availability). These are reasonable but should be flagged as "Additional Assumptions" to avoid confusion with given constraints.
- **Correction:** Rename Section 3 to "Additional Assumptions and Scope Boundaries."

**7. Team Responsibilities (Unspecified FTE Allocation)**
- **Claim:** 0.5 FTE Data Analyst, 0.5 FTE Support Lead, 0.25 FTE Privacy/Legal.
- **Evidence:** The plan uses these FTEs but does not specify how the 0.5 FTE Data Analyst is split (e.g., 2.5 days/week). This is acceptable as a high-level plan.
- **No issue.**

**8. Final Decision Framework (Missing "Limited Extension" Detail)**
- **Claim:** "Limited Extension or Revision" for 3-4 metrics met.
- **Evidence:** The framework does not specify the duration of the extension (4 weeks is mentioned in the table but not in the text). The disruption plan mentions 1-week extension. Inconsistency. **Correction:** Align extension duration across sections.

**Summary of Required Corrections:**
1. Recalculate agent stipend budget to reflect actual pilot duration (~$1,280, not $10,000). Adjust total budget accordingly or reallocate.
2. Justify or reduce pilot volume from 5,000 to ~800 requests/month based on agent hours.
3. Clarify go/no-go rule: safety is hard gate; other metrics allow revision.
4. Label assumptions as "Additional Assumptions."
5. Align extension duration between decision framework and disruption plan.

**Unresolved Issues:**
- No information on existing ticketing system API capabilities—assumed but critical.
- No cost estimate for model training (GPU/TPU)—$60,000 may be insufficient for fine-tuning a large LLM.
- No contingency for privacy/legal specialist being unavailable at 0.25 FTE.

**Recommendation:** Approve with corrections noted. Proceed to implementation after budget recalculation.

3. **Safety & Compliance**: Never expose payment-card data; never auto-approve refunds, account changes, or payment disputes.
4. **Budget Discipline**: Spend exactly $180,000; prioritize essential infrastructure if reduced.
5. **Evidence-Based Decision**: Use pilot data to launch, revise, or cancel by Week 12.

## 3. Assumptions and Scope Boundaries

**Assumptions:**
- Existing support ticketing system has API access for integration.
- Historical data (anonymized) is available for model training.

## 4. Twelve-Week Phased Roadmap

| **Phase 4: Pilot (Weeks 9-10)** | Live pilot with 8 agents handling ~5,000 requests/month; collect metrics; weekly reviews. | Weekly metric reports; agent feedback logs. | PM, Data Analyst, Support Lead, ML Eng | Pilot runs for 2 full weeks; data collected. |
| **Phase 5: Decision (Weeks 11-12)** | Analyze pilot data; compare to thresholds; prepare recommendation; final go/no-go decision. | Final analysis report; decision document. | PM, Data Analyst, Privacy/Legal, ML Eng | Decision made by end of Week 12. |

## 5. Team Responsibilities

- **Product Manager (1 FTE)**: Overall coordination, stakeholder communication, budget tracking, decision framework.
- **Machine Learning Engineer (1 FTE)**: Model training, evaluation, safety guardrails, performance tuning.
- **Backend Engineer (1 FTE)**: API development, system integration, data pipeline, security implementation.
- **Data Analyst (0.5 FTE)**: Metric collection, statistical analysis, dashboard creation, pilot reporting.
- **Customer Support Lead (0.5 FTE)**: Agent training, workflow design, feedback collection, escalation management.
- **Privacy & Legal Specialist (0.25 FTE)**: Compliance review, data anonymization audit, safety rule approval.

## 6. Budget Allocation

| Budget Category | Amount | Purpose | Priority if Budget Reduced |
|---|---|---|---|
| Cloud Compute & Model Training | $60,000 | GPU/TPU time, model hosting, data storage | High (essential for development) |
| Engineering Salaries (ML + Backend) | $70,000 | 12 weeks of 2 engineers | High (core team) |
| PM & Analyst Salaries | $30,000 | 12 weeks of PM + 0.5 analyst | Medium (can reduce analyst hours) |
| Agent Training & Testing Stipends | $10,000 | 8 agents × 2 hrs/week × 12 weeks | Low (can reduce to 1 hr/week) |
| Privacy/Legal Consulting | $5,000 | 0.25 FTE for 12 weeks | Low (can defer some reviews) |
| Contingency Reserve | $5,000 | Unforeseen costs | Medium (use only if needed) |
| **Total** | **$180,000** | | |

- **Scale**: 8 agents handle ~5,000 requests/month (10% of volume) during Weeks 9-10.

- **Monitoring**: Daily CSAT surveys, response-time tracking, agent workload logs, safety violation alerts.
- **Duration**: 2 weeks live (Weeks 9-10) with data collection; analysis in Weeks 11-12.

## 8. Success Metrics and Decision Thresholds

| Metric | Baseline | Target | Measurement Method | Go/No-Go Threshold |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Avg First-Response Time | 18 hours | ≤8 hours | Ticketing system timestamps | ≤8 hours (Go); >8 hours (No-Go) |
| Customer Satisfaction | 78% | ≥84% | Post-interaction survey (1-5 scale) | ≥84% (Go); <84% (No-Go) |
| Repetitive Query Automation | 0% | ≥40% | % of queries where AI draft used without major edits | ≥40% (Go); <40% (No-Go) |
| Safety Incidents | 0 | 0 | Manual audit of all AI-drafted responses | 0 incidents (Go); any incident (No-Go) |

|---|---|---|

The final decision framework provides clear criteria for launch, revision, or cancellation, protecting the company from unsafe or ineffective deployment.

This 12-week strategy pilots an AI-powered customer-support assistant for a mid-sized e-commerce company handling 50,000 monthly requests. The pilot aims to reduce first-response time from 18 hours to ≤8 hours, raise customer satisfaction from 78% to ≥84%, and reduce repetitive agent workload—all within a $180,000 budget and strict safety/privacy constraints. A final go/no-go decision is made by Week 12.

## 2. Strategic Objectives and Priorities

1. **Speed & Satisfaction**: Achieve ≤8-hour first response and ≥84% CSAT.
2. **Agent Relief**: Automate ≥40% of repetitive queries (e.g., order status, return policies).
3. **Safety & Compliance**: Never expose payment-card data; never auto-approve refunds, account changes, or payment disputes.
4. **Budget Discipline**: Spend exactly $180,000; prioritize essential infrastructure if reduced.
5. **Evidence-Based Decision**: Use pilot data to launch, revise, or cancel by Week 12.

## 3. Assumptions and Scope Boundaries

**Assumptions:**
- Existing support ticketing system has API access for integration.
- Historical data (anonymized) is available for model training.
- Agents can be trained on new tool within 2 hours/week.
- Privacy/Legal specialist can review outputs within 0.25 FTE.
- Budget reduction means cutting non-essential items; core development continues.

**Scope Boundaries:**
- AI may draft responses and recommend actions only.
- No independent refunds, account modifications, or payment dispute resolution.
- Payment-card numbers stripped from all model inputs.
- Pilot limited to 8 agents, 2 hours/week each.
- Pilot must start by end of Week 8; final decision by end of Week 12.

## 4. Twelve-Week Phased Roadmap

| Phase & Weeks | Main Activities | Deliverables | Responsible Roles | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Phase 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-3)** | Define scope; collect & anonymize 10,000 historical tickets; build data pipeline; draft safety rules; select AI model (e.g., fine-tuned LLM). | Data pipeline; safety rules document; model selection report. | PM, ML Eng, Backend Eng, Data Analyst, Privacy/Legal | Data pipeline tested; safety rules approved; model selected. |
| **Phase 2: Development (Weeks 4-6)** | Train model on anonymized data; build API for draft responses; integrate with ticketing system; create agent dashboard; unit tests. | Trained model; integration API; agent dashboard v1; test results. | ML Eng, Backend Eng, PM, Data Analyst | Model accuracy ≥80% on test set; integration passes security review. |
| **Phase 3: Pilot Prep (Weeks 7-8)** | Train 8 agents (2 hrs each); set up monitoring; define escalation paths; run internal dry-run with synthetic queries. | Agent training completed; monitoring dashboard; dry-run report. | PM, Support Lead, ML Eng, Privacy/Legal | Dry-run shows no safety violations; agents comfortable with tool. |

- **Machine Learning Engineer (1 FTE)**: Model training, evaluation, safety guardrails, performance tuning.
- **Backend Engineer (1 FTE)**: API development, system integration, data pipeline, security implementation.
- **Data Analyst (0.5 FTE)**: Metric collection, statistical analysis, dashboard creation, pilot reporting.
- **Customer Support Lead (0.5 FTE)**: Agent training, workflow design, feedback collection, escalation management.
- **Privacy & Legal Specialist (0.25 FTE)**: Compliance review, data anonymization audit, safety rule approval.

| Budget Category | Amount | Purpose | Priority if Budget Reduced |
|---|---|---|---|

| Engineering Salaries (ML + Backend) | $70,000 | 12 weeks of 2 engineers | High (core team) |
| PM & Analyst Salaries | $30,000 | 12 weeks of PM + 0.5 analyst | Medium (can reduce analyst hours) |
| Agent Training & Testing Stipends | $10,000 | 8 agents × 2 hrs/week × 12 weeks | Low (can reduce to 1 hr/week) |
| Privacy/Legal Consulting | $5,000 | 0.25 FTE for 12 weeks | Low (can defer some reviews) |
| Contingency Reserve | $5,000 | Unforeseen costs | Medium (use only if needed) |

## 7. Pilot Design

- **Scale**: 8 agents handle ~5,000 requests/month (10% of volume) during Weeks 9-10.
- **Process**: AI drafts response → agent reviews/edits → sends. Agent logs satisfaction with draft.
- **Safety**: Model inputs stripped of payment-card data via regex + PII detection. All refund/account/payment disputes routed to human-only queue.
- **Monitoring**: Daily CSAT surveys, response-time tracking, agent workload logs, safety violation alerts.
- **Duration**: 2 weeks live (Weeks 9-10) with data collection; analysis in Weeks 11-12.

## 8. Success Metrics and Decision Thresholds

| Metric | Baseline | Target | Measurement Method | Go/No-Go Threshold |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Avg First-Response Time | 18 hours | ≤8 hours | Ticketing system timestamps | ≤8 hours (Go); >8 hours (No-Go) |

| Safety Incidents | 0 | 0 | Manual audit of all AI-drafted responses | 0 incidents (Go); any incident (No-Go) |
| Agent Time Saved | 0 | ≥2 hrs/week/agent | Self-reported time logs | ≥2 hrs (Go); <2 hrs (No-Go) |

| Agent resistance to tool | Medium | Medium | Low adoption in first week | Training; feedback sessions; show time savings | Support Lead |
| Integration delays | Medium | High | API testing fails | Buffer 1 week in Phase 2; use simpler integration | Backend Eng |
| Model accuracy <80% | Medium | High | Test set performance low | More training data; fine-tuning; simpler model | ML Eng |
| Pilot scale too small for statistical significance | Low | Medium | High variance in metrics | Use Bayesian analysis; extend pilot to 3 weeks if needed | Data Analyst |

## 10. Disruption-Response Plans

**Disruption 1: Budget reduced 20% at end of Week 6**
- **Immediate action**: PM notifies team; implement pre-planned cuts (see Budget section).
- **Impact**: Reduce cloud compute (use smaller model), cut analyst to 0.25 FTE, reduce agent training to 1 hr/week.
- **Timeline**: Adjustments made by Week 7 start; no delay to pilot start.
- **Contingency**: If cuts threaten model quality, use pre-trained model instead of fine-tuning.

**Disruption 2: Pilot shows faster response times but CSAT <84% at end of Week 9**
- **Immediate action**: Data Analyst runs root-cause analysis (e.g., generic responses, errors).
- **Response**: Extend pilot by 1 week (Week 10 becomes Week 11); adjust model tone/persona; add agent training on AI editing.
- **Decision**: If CSAT improves to ≥84% by end of extended pilot, proceed to launch. If not, recommend revision (e.g., more agent training) or cancellation.
- **Timeline**: Final decision moved to end of Week 12 (original deadline maintained).

## 11. Final Decision Framework

| Condition | Decision | Action |
|---|---|---|
| All 5 metrics meet thresholds | **Launch** | Full rollout plan; allocate remaining budget for scaling |
| 3-4 metrics met (including safety) | **Limited Extension or Revision** | 4-week extension to fix gaps; re-evaluate with new data |
| ≤2 metrics met, or any safety incident | **Cancel** | Document lessons; archive model; reallocate team |
| Budget cut caused metric failure | **Revision** | Seek additional funding or scope reduction; re-pilot |

**Decision Authority**: PM, with sign-off from Privacy/Legal and Support Lead.

## 12. Conclusion

This 12-week strategy balances speed, quality, safety, and budget. By phasing preparation, development, pilot, and decision-making, the team can deliver evidence-based results by Week 12. Built-in disruption responses ensure resilience. The final decision framework provides clear criteria for launch, revision, or cancellation, protecting the company from unsafe or ineffective deployment.

- **Process**: AI drafts response → agent reviews/edits → sends. Agent logs satisfaction with draft.
- **Safety**: Model inputs stripped of payment-card data via regex + PII detection. All refund/account/payment disputes routed to human-only queue.
- **Monitoring**: Daily CSAT surveys, response-time tracking, agent workload logs, safety violation alerts.
- **Duration**: 2 weeks live (Weeks 9-10) with data collection; analysis in Weeks 11-12.

## 8. Success Metrics and Decision Thresholds

| Metric | Baseline | Target | Measurement Method | Go/No-Go Threshold |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Avg First-Response Time | 18 hours | ≤8 hours | Ticketing system timestamps | ≤8 hours (Go); >8 hours (No-Go) |
| Customer Satisfaction | 78% | ≥84% | Post-interaction survey (1-5 scale) | ≥84% (Go); <84% (No-Go) |
| Repetitive Query Automation | 0% | ≥40% | % of queries where AI draft used without major edits | ≥40% (Go); <40% (No-Go) |
| Safety Incidents | 0 | 0 | Manual audit of all AI-drafted responses | 0 incidents (Go); any incident (No-Go) |
| Agent Time Saved | 0 | ≥2 hrs/week/agent | Self-reported time logs | ≥2 hrs (Go); <2 hrs (No-Go) |

**Go/No-Go Rule**: All five thresholds must be met for launch. If 3-4 met, consider limited extension/revision. If ≤2 met, cancel.

## 9. Risk Register

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Early Warning Sign | Mitigation | Contingency Owner |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Model produces unsafe responses | Medium | High | Safety audit flags errors | Strict guardrails; human-in-loop; weekly audits | ML Eng |
| Budget cut at Week 6 | High | Medium | Finance notification | Pre-planned cost reductions (see budget) | PM |
| Pilot fails CSAT target | Medium | High | Weekly CSAT <80% by Week 9 | Analyze root cause; extend pilot 1 week; adjust model | Data Analyst |
| Data privacy breach | Low | Critical | PII detected in model inputs | Strip PII pre-input; encrypt data; legal review | Privacy/Legal |
| Agent resistance to tool | Medium | Medium | Low adoption in first week | Training; feedback sessions; show time savings | Support Lead |
| Integration delays | Medium | High | API testing fails | Buffer 1 week in Phase 2; use simpler integration | Backend Eng |
| Model accuracy <80% | Medium | High | Test set performance low | More training data; fine-tuning; simpler model | ML Eng |
| Pilot scale too small for statistical significance | Low | Medium | High variance in metrics | Use Bayesian analysis; extend pilot to 3 weeks if needed | Data Analyst |

## 10. Disruption-Response Plans

**Disruption 1: Budget reduced 20% at end of Week 6**
- **Immediate action**: PM notifies team; implement pre-planned cuts (see Budget section).
- **Impact**: Reduce cloud compute (use smaller model), cut analyst to 0.25 FTE, reduce agent training to 1 hr/week.
- **Timeline**: Adjustments made by Week 7 start; no delay to pilot start.
- **Contingency**: If cuts threaten model quality, use pre-trained model instead of fine-tuning.

**Disruption 2: Pilot shows faster response times but CSAT <84% at end of Week 9**
- **Immediate action**: Data Analyst runs root-cause analysis (e.g., generic responses, errors).
- **Response**: Extend pilot by 1 week (Week 10 becomes Week 11); adjust model tone/persona; add agent training on AI editing.
- **Decision**: If CSAT improves to ≥84% by end of extended pilot, proceed to launch. If not, recommend revision (e.g., more agent training) or cancellation.
- **Timeline**: Final decision moved to end of Week 12 (original deadline maintained).

## 11. Final Decision Framework

| Condition | Decision | Action |
|---|---|---|
| All 5 metrics meet thresholds | **Launch** | Full rollout plan; allocate remaining budget for scaling |
| 3-4 metrics met (including safety) | **Limited Extension or Revision** | 4-week extension to fix gaps; re-evaluate with new data |
| ≤2 metrics met, or any safety incident | **Cancel** | Document lessons; archive model; reallocate team |
| Budget cut caused metric failure | **Revision** | Seek additional funding or scope reduction; re-pilot |

**Decision Authority**: PM, with sign-off from Privacy/Legal and Support Lead.

## 12. Conclusion

This 12-week strategy balances speed, quality, safety, and budget. By phasing preparation, development, pilot, and decision-making, the team can deliver evidence-based results by Week 12. Built-in disruption responses ensure resilience. The final decision framework provides clear criteria for launch, revision, or cancellation, protecting the company from unsafe or ineffective deployment.

| Contingency Reserve | $5,000 | Unforeseen costs | Medium (use only if needed) |
| **Total** | **$180,000** | | |

*If budget reduced 20% ($36,000 cut):* Cut Contingency ($5,000), reduce Agent Stipends to $5,000, cut Analyst to 0.25 FTE (saving $15,000), reduce Engineering by $11,000 (e.g., fewer cloud credits).

## 7. Pilot Design

- **Scale**: 8 agents handle ~5,000 requests/month (10% of volume) during Weeks 9-10.
- **Process**: AI drafts response → agent reviews/edits → sends. Agent logs satisfaction with draft.
- **Safety**: Model inputs stripped of payment-card data via regex + PII detection. All refund/account/payment disputes routed to human-only queue.
- **Monitoring**: Daily CSAT surveys, response-time tracking, agent workload logs, safety violation alerts.
- **Duration**: 2 weeks live (Weeks 9-10) with data collection; analysis in Weeks 11-12.

## 8. Success Metrics and Decision Thresholds

| Metric | Baseline | Target | Measurement Method | Go/No-Go Threshold |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Avg First-Response Time | 18 hours | ≤8 hours | Ticketing system timestamps | ≤8 hours (Go); >8 hours (No-Go) |
| Customer Satisfaction | 78% | ≥84% | Post-interaction survey (1-5 scale) | ≥84% (Go); <84% (No-Go) |
| Repetitive Query Automation | 0% | ≥40% | % of queries where AI draft used without major edits | ≥40% (Go); <40% (No-Go) |
| Safety Incidents | 0 | 0 | Manual audit of all AI-drafted responses | 0 incidents (Go); any incident (No-Go) |
| Agent Time Saved | 0 | ≥2 hrs/week/agent | Self-reported time logs | ≥2 hrs (Go); <2 hrs (No-Go) |

**Go/No-Go Rule**: All five thresholds must be met for launch. If 3-4 met, consider limited extension/revision. If ≤2 met, cancel.

## 9. Risk Register

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Early Warning Sign | Mitigation | Contingency Owner |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Model produces unsafe responses | Medium | High | Safety audit flags errors | Strict guardrails; human-in-loop; weekly audits | ML Eng |
| Budget cut at Week 6 | High | Medium | Finance notification | Pre-planned cost reductions (see budget) | PM |
| Pilot fails CSAT target | Medium | High | Weekly CSAT <80% by Week 9 | Analyze root cause; extend pilot 1 week; adjust model | Data Analyst |
| Data privacy breach | Low | Critical | PII detected in model inputs | Strip PII pre-input; encrypt data; legal review | Privacy/Legal |
| Agent resistance to tool | Medium | Medium | Low adoption in first week | Training; feedback sessions; show time savings | Support Lead |
| Integration delays | Medium | High | API testing fails | Buffer 1 week in Phase 2; use simpler integration | Backend Eng |
| Model accuracy <80% | Medium | High | Test set performance low | More training data; fine-tuning; simpler model | ML Eng |
| Pilot scale too small for statistical significance | Low | Medium | High variance in metrics | Use Bayesian analysis; extend pilot to 3 weeks if needed | Data Analyst |

## 10. Disruption-Response Plans

**Disruption 1: Budget reduced 20% at end of Week 6**
- **Immediate action**: PM notifies team; implement pre-planned cuts (see Budget section).
- **Impact**: Reduce cloud compute (use smaller model), cut analyst to 0.25 FTE, reduce agent training to 1 hr/week.
- **Timeline**: Adjustments made by Week 7 start; no delay to pilot start.
- **Contingency**: If cuts threaten model quality, use pre-trained model instead of fine-tuning.

**Disruption 2: Pilot shows faster response times but CSAT <84% at end of Week 9**
- **Immediate action**: Data Analyst runs root-cause analysis (e.g., generic responses, errors).
- **Response**: Extend pilot by 1 week (Week 10 becomes Week 11); adjust model tone/persona; add agent training on AI editing.
- **Decision**: If CSAT improves to ≥84% by end of extended pilot, proceed to launch. If not, recommend revision (e.g., more agent training) or cancellation.
- **Timeline**: Final decision moved to end of Week 12 (original deadline maintained).

## 11. Final Decision Framework

| Condition | Decision | Action |
|---|---|---|
| All 5 metrics meet thresholds | **Launch** | Full rollout plan; allocate remaining budget for scaling |
| 3-4 metrics met (including safety) | **Limited Extension or Revision** | 4-week extension to fix gaps; re-evaluate with new data |
| ≤2 metrics met, or any safety incident | **Cancel** | Document lessons; archive model; reallocate team |
| Budget cut caused metric failure | **Revision** | Seek additional funding or scope reduction; re-pilot |

**Decision Authority**: PM, with sign-off from Privacy/Legal and Support Lead.

## 12. Conclusion

This 12-week strategy balances speed, quality, safety, and budget. By phasing preparation, development, pilot, and decision-making, the team can deliver evidence-based results by Week 12. Built-in disruption responses ensure resilience. The final decision framework provides clear criteria for launch, revision, or cancellation, protecting the company from unsafe or ineffective deployment.

The plan phases preparation, development, pilot, and decision-making, with built-in responses to two potential disruptions: a 20% budget cut at Week 6 and a satisfaction shortfall at Week 9. A final go/no-go decision is made by Week 12.

## 2. Strategic Objectives and Priorities

1. **Speed & Satisfaction**: Achieve ≤8-hour first response and ≥84% CSAT.
2. **Agent Relief**: Automate ≥40% of repetitive queries (e.g., order status, return policies).
3. **Safety & Compliance**: Never expose payment-card data; never auto-approve refunds, account changes, or payment disputes.
4. **Budget Discipline**: Spend exactly $180,000; prioritize essential infrastructure if reduced.
5. **Evidence-Based Decision**: Use pilot data to launch, revise, or cancel by Week 12.

## 3. Additional Assumptions and Scope Boundaries

**Additional Assumptions:**
- Existing support ticketing system has API access for integration.
- Historical data (anonymized) is available for model training.
- Agents can be trained on new tool within 2 hours/week.
- Privacy/Legal specialist can review outputs within 0.25 FTE.
- Budget reduction means cutting non-essential items; core development continues.

**Scope Boundaries:**
- AI may draft responses and recommend actions only.
- No independent refunds, account modifications, or payment dispute resolution.
- Payment-card numbers stripped from all model inputs.
- Pilot limited to 8 agents, 2 hours/week each.
- Pilot must start by end of Week 8; final decision by end of Week 12.

## 4. Twelve-Week Phased Roadmap

| Phase & Weeks | Main Activities | Deliverables | Responsible Roles | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Phase 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-3)** | Define scope; collect & anonymize 10,000 historical tickets; build data pipeline; draft safety rules; select AI model (e.g., fine-tuned LLM). | Data pipeline; safety rules document; model selection report. | PM, ML Eng, Backend Eng, Data Analyst, Privacy/Legal | Data pipeline tested; safety rules approved; model selected. |
| **Phase 2: Development (Weeks 4-6)** | Train model on anonymized data; build API for draft responses; integrate with ticketing system; create agent dashboard; unit tests. | Trained model; integration API; agent dashboard v1; test results. | ML Eng, Backend Eng, PM, Data Analyst | Model accuracy ≥80% on test set; integration passes security review. |
| **Phase 3: Pilot Prep (Weeks 7-8)** | Train 8 agents (2 hrs each); set up monitoring; define escalation paths; run internal dry-run with synthetic queries. | Agent training completed; monitoring dashboard; dry-run report. | PM, Support Lead, ML Eng, Privacy/Legal | Dry-run shows no safety violations; agents comfortable with tool. |
| **Phase 4: Pilot (Weeks 9-10)** | Live pilot with 8 agents handling ~2,000 requests/month; collect metrics; weekly reviews. | Weekly metric reports; agent feedback logs. | PM, Data Analyst, Support Lead, ML Eng | Pilot runs for 2 full weeks; data collected. |
| **Phase 5: Decision (Weeks 11-12)** | Analyze pilot data; compare to thresholds; prepare recommendation; final go/no-go decision. | Final analysis report; decision document. | PM, Data Analyst, Privacy/Legal, ML Eng | Decision made by end of Week 12. |

## 5. Team Responsibilities

- **Product Manager (1 FTE)**: Overall coordination, stakeholder communication, budget tracking, decision framework.
- **Machine Learning Engineer (1 FTE)**: Model training, evaluation, safety guardrails, performance tuning.
- **Backend Engineer (1 FTE)**: API development, system integration, data pipeline, security implementation.
- **Data Analyst (0.5 FTE)**: Metric collection, statistical analysis, dashboard creation, pilot reporting.
- **Customer Support Lead (0.5 FTE)**: Agent training, workflow design, feedback collection, escalation management.
- **Privacy & Legal Specialist (0.25 FTE)**: Compliance review, data anonymization audit, safety rule approval.

## 6. Budget Allocation

Create a 12-week strategy for piloting and deciding whether to launch an AI-powered customer-support assistant for a mid-sized e-commerce company.

The company currently handles approximately 50,000 customer-support requests per month.

Current performance:
- Average first-response time: 18 hours
- Customer satisfaction score: 78%
- Support agents frequently spend time answering repetitive questions
- Refunds, account changes, and payment disputes require human approval

The company wants the pilot to achieve the following goals:
- Reduce average first-response time to 8 hours or less
- Increase customer satisfaction to at least 84%
- Reduce repetitive workload for support agents
- Avoid unsafe or unauthorized actions
- Protect customer and payment-related information

The project has the following constraints:
- Total budget: $180,000
- Total duration: 12 weeks
- A limited pilot must begin by the end of Week 8
- A final launch, revision, or cancellation decision must be made by the end of Week 12
- The system may draft responses and recommend actions, but it may not independently issue refunds, modify accounts, or resolve payment disputes
- Payment-card information must not be included in model inputs
- Project team: 1 Product Manager, 1 Machine Learning Engineer, 1 Backend Engineer, 0.5 Full-Time Data Analyst, 0.5 Full-Time Customer Support Lead, and 0.25 Full-Time Privacy and Legal Specialist
- Eight customer-support agents may participate in testing for no more than two hours per person per week

Develop a practical strategy that balances delivery speed, service quality, employee workload, privacy, safety, and budget.

The plan must also address two possible disruptions:
1. At the end of Week 6, the available budget may be reduced by 20%.
2. At the end of Week 9, the pilot may show faster response times but fail to reach the customer-satisfaction target.

The response must include: an executive summary; strategic priorities; assumptions and scope boundaries; a complete 12-week roadmap; team responsibilities; a budget totaling exactly $180,000; pilot design; success metrics and go/no-go thresholds; a risk register with at least six risks; disruption-response plans; and a final decision framework distinguishing launch, limited extension or revision, and cancellation.

Use the following required tables:
1. Roadmap: Phase and Weeks | Main Activities | Deliverables | Responsible Roles | Exit Criteria

3. Success Metrics: Metric | Baseline | Target | Measurement Method | Go/No-Go Threshold

The response should be approximately 1,000-1,400 words. Use only the information and constraints provided. Clearly label any additional assumptions. Do not invent completed pilot results or claim that the strategy has already been tested.
